raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":0.7,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"A general review of the section centrally involves checking whether the argument moves coherently from motivation to benchmark results to conclusion.","R1.3":"The section makes quantitative performance and bias-reduction claims, so review should assess whether those claims are supported by the reported metrics and comparisons.","R1.6":"Because the delegation asks to review a written section, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and flow are central expected responsibilities.","RX.1":"The projection should preserve uncertainty about what kind of review is intended and avoid implying unavailable validation beyond the artifact.","RX.2":"The section contains strong claims such as survey-agnostic effectiveness and confirmation, making overclaim avoidance actively relevant.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting review responsibilities and not perform the review or alter the artifact.","RX.4":"Review responsibilities should include avoiding guidance that could amplify misleading scientific claims or unsupported downstream use.","RX.5":"Any projected responsibility should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the specific claims present in the artifact."}}

rationale.R1.2: A general review of the section centrally involves checking whether the argument moves coherently from motivation to benchmark results to conclusion.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes quantitative performance and bias-reduction claims, so review should assess whether those claims are supported by the reported metrics and comparisons.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation asks to review a written section, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and flow are central expected responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: The projection should preserve uncertainty about what kind of review is intended and avoid implying unavailable validation beyond the artifact.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains strong claims such as survey-agnostic effectiveness and confirmation, making overclaim avoidance actively relevant.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting review responsibilities and not perform the review or alter the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Review responsibilities should include avoiding guidance that could amplify misleading scientific claims or unsupported downstream use.
rationale.RX.5: Any projected responsibility should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the specific claims present in the artifact.
